description: Recommended sequential study path through all courses. Each course lists prerequisite and next course. Students may begin OT Deep Study after Hermeneutics I without completing all NT courses first.

primary_path.title: Biblical Foundations I
primary_path.rationale: Entry point. Establishes the covenant framework and genre categories required for all subsequent study.

primary_path.title: Hermeneutics I
primary_path.rationale: Builds the historical-grammatical method skills applied in all NT and OT text-based courses.

primary_path.title: NT Foundations — Matthew
primary_path.rationale: First NT text-based course. Matthew's fulfillment structure rewards students who understand the OT covenant framework from BF01.

primary_path.title: NT Foundations — Mark
primary_path.rationale: Mark's urgency and Messianic Secret are understood better after Matthew's fulfillment framework.

primary_path.title: NT Foundations — Luke
primary_path.rationale: Luke's orderly account and social themes build on synoptic familiarity.

primary_path.title: NT Foundations — John
primary_path.rationale: John's theological depth is best approached after the three synoptics have established the narrative.

primary_path.title: NT Foundations — Acts
primary_path.rationale: Acts follows directly from Luke and provides historical context for all the Pauline letters.

primary_path.title: NT Foundations — Pauline Epistles
primary_path.rationale: Paul's letters are situated historically by Acts. Romans through Philemon in canonical order.

primary_path.title: NT Foundations — General Epistles and Revelation
primary_path.rationale: Completes the NT canon. Revelation is best understood after familiarity with the full NT theological framework.

primary_path.title: Old Testament Deep Study
primary_path.rationale: Can be taken after Hermeneutics I at any point. Covering the full OT in canonical order is best done with the hermeneutical tools established in BF01 and Hermeneutics I.

alternate_path.description: Students wishing to study the OT before completing all NT courses may take OT Deep Study after Hermeneutics I, then return to NT Matthew.
